purpose: Govern mandatory turn-end writeback checks, conditional persistence, and recompilation for Meta-Runtime-Memory.
instruction: At turn end, check whether this turn produced durable signal for user memory, task memory, or both.
Write back only stable signal that affects long-term goal, current state, or stable behavior constraints.
If no durable signal exists, skip snapshot writeback but still persist a turn audit record with writeback_decision=skipped.
When writeback occurs, append a concise turn delta and recompile active memory.
workflow: Classify new information as durable, transient, or uncertain.
Update USER_MEMORY.json only for stable user-layer signals.
Update TASK_MEMORY.json only for durable task-layer signals.
Append a turn delta entry that records what changed and why; skipped turns still require a persisted audit trace.
Write TURN_AUDIT.json with session_id, turn_id, changed_paths, validation_runs, and writeback_decision.
Re-render Markdown mirrors from JSON source of truth.
Recompile ACTIVE_MEMORY.json for the next turn.
rules: Turn-end check is mandatory even when no writeback occurs.
Skipped turn-end checks still need durable audit evidence.
Do not write back raw transcript or tool output.
Do not let turn delta replace the current snapshot.
Uncertain signal stays out until clarified.
